impact:
V1E THD rises with frequency on the pedal but FALLS on the plugin — the slope has the wrong sign:
            100Hz  200Hz  400Hz
  pedal      4.5    10.5   22.3   <- RISES
  plugin     5.9     3.6    0.9   <- FALLS
NEW (2026-07-16, comprehensive_data.json, full-band Farina): the pedal keeps climbing well past the previously-anchored 400Hz — ~31% @1016Hz and ~35% @1280Hz (D0.50, drv-12) vs the plugin's ~1.5%/3.4%. The gap is far larger and wider than the audit recorded. THD is a RATIO so this data is immune to the capture level-normalization and needed no gain-match fix.

CANDIDATE 1 IS NOW RULED OUT BY DATA. The audit's first candidate was "the twin-T notch is upstream of the clip — if the real notch is shallower/narrower than modelled, the pedal would keep driving the clip". cascade_analysis.py shows the twin-T notch region (600-1000Hz) matches within ~1.2dB at LOW drive on V1E (D0.50 -1.11dB, D0.60 -1.19dB) and only diverges at high drive (D1.00 -11.41dB). A filter error would be present at EVERY drive. The notch filter is therefore CORRECT; the drive-dependence is the fault. Do not re-investigate the notch component values.

This makes the remaining cause drive/clip-stage behaviour, SHARED with ISS-002 (gap B) and ISS-004 (gap D): V2 shows the same drive-dependent twin-T divergence (D0.25 +4.27 -> D0.90 -2.75, swing 7.03dB). Solve once, not three times. Note the sign is opposite between revisions, so it is not a single shared constant.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/gap_audit.py --mode thd --rev V1E ; python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section B).
title: V1E THD-vs-frequency slope has wrong sign
